title: Getting Started with DemiEngine
description: Quick start guide and basic usage for DemiEngine virtual machine

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: DemiEngine is a revolutionary virtual machine with 134 registers, 63 implemented opcodes, and 100% test coverage. It serves as the foundation for the upcoming Demi programming language - a fully customizable programming environment where you can tailor syntax, semantics, and behavior to your exact needs.

sections.title: Prerequisites
sections.content:
- C++17 compatible compiler (g++ 7.0 or later)
- Make build system
- Git for cloning the repository
- Linux, macOS, or WSL2 (Windows)

sections.title: Installation
sections.content:
```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/bobrossrtx/demi.git
cd demi

# Build DemiEngine
make

# Verify installation
./bin/demi-engine --help
```

sections.title: Running Your First Program
sections.content:
```bash
# Run a hello world example
./bin/demi-engine tests/hex/helloworld.hex

# Run with debug output
./bin/demi-engine tests/hex/helloworld.hex --debug

# Show extended register output
./bin/demi-engine tests/hex/helloworld.hex --extended-registers
```

sections.title: Using Assembly Mode
sections.content:
DemiEngine includes a powerful assembly language with test directives:

```bash
# Assemble and run .asm files
./bin/demi-engine -A examples/hello_world.asm

# Run in-assembly tests
./bin/demi-engine -at

# Show only failed tests
./bin/demi-engine -at --show=fails

# Show only passed tests
./bin/demi-engine -at --show=success
```

sections.title: Command-Line Options
sections.content:
```
--help, -h              Show help information
--debug, -d             Enable debug mode with detailed logging
--verbose, -v           Show informational messages
--extended-registers    Show all 134 registers
--hex, -H              Run hex file
--assembly, -A         Assemble and run .asm file
--assembly-test, -at   Run in-assembly tests
--show=MODE            Filter test output (all|fails|success)
--compile, -o          Compile to standalone executable
```

sections.title: Next Steps
sections.content:
- Read the [Instruction Set Reference](/docs/instruction-set) to learn available opcodes
- Explore [Programming Examples](/docs/examples) for common patterns
- Check out the [Assembly Language Guide](/docs/assembly) for writing .asm files
- Learn about [Device I/O](/docs/devices) for interacting with virtual hardware
